message.content:
You are working in /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service (NestJS/TypeScript monorepo).

## Your Task
Fix ESLint `custom-rules/require-object-params` violations for Jira ticket RECO-696.
Ticket URL: https://customink.atlassian.net/browse/RECO-696

## Context
- Current branch: `RECO-688-fix-style-alternatives-styles-mv-require-object-params`
- You must create a NEW branch from this branch (NOT from main)
- Branch name format: `RECO-696-<kebab-summary>`
- Commit format: `RECO-696: <imperative description>`

## ESLint Rule
`custom-rules/require-object-params` ÔÇö all exported functions must use a single object destructuring parameter instead of positional params.

**Pattern:**
```typescript
// ÔŁî Before
export function myFn(id: string, name: string): void {}
// Ôťů After
export function myFn({ id, name }: { id: string; name: string }): void {}
```

## Steps

### 1. Find the target file
Run ESLint to find remaining files with violations:
```bash
npx eslint --max-warnings=0 src/ --format=compact 2>&1 | grep "require-object-params" | head -40
```
Pick ALL remaining files with violations ÔÇö this is the last ticket, so fix everything that's left.

### 2. Create branch
```bash
git checkout -b RECO-696-<descriptive-name>
```
Branch from current branch `RECO-688-fix-style-alternatives-styles-mv-require-object-params`.

### 3. Fix violations in target file(s)
- Change positional params to `{ param }: { param: type }` destructuring
- Update ALL internal calls to changed functions
- Find ALL callers: use Grep tool to search for function names across src/ and test/
- Update every call site
- Do NOT use `{ ...param }` spread shortcuts

### 4. Update tests
Find and update all test files that call changed functions.

### 5. Run tests
```bash
npm run test:unit -- --testPathPatterns="<pattern>" 2>&1
```

### 6. Run type-check
```bash
npm run type-check 2>&1
```

### 7. Verify ESLint
```bash
npx eslint --max-warnings=0 <changed-files> 2>&1
```

### 8. Commit (only relevant files ÔÇö NOT .husky/pre-push)
```bash
git add <specific-files-only>
git commit -m "RECO-696: <description>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>"
```

### 9. Push
```bash
git push -u origin RECO-696-<branch-name>
```

### 10. Create DRAFT PR
Base = `RECO-688-fix-style-alternatives-styles-mv-require-object-params` (NOT main).

```bash
gh pr create --draft --base RECO-688-fix-style-alternatives-styles-mv-require-object-params --title "RECO-696: ..." --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
### Ticket ­čÉŚ

JIRA task: [RECO-696](https://customink.atlassian.net/browse/RECO-696)
Design: N/A

### Changes ­čŹŹ

<describe changes>

### PR Checks ­čÉŐ

- [x] I've self-reviewed my code.
- [x] I've added the necessary labels.
- [x] I've added tests for my code.
- [ ] I've updated the documentation.

### Description / Ticket Summary ­čÉÖ

Fix ESLint custom-rules/require-object-params violations ÔÇö refactored functions to use single object destructuring parameter pattern.

### Screenshots / Short Videos ­čÉ«

N/A
EOF
)"
```

## Critical Rules
- NEVER modify `.husky/pre-push`
- NEVER use relative imports (use `@utils/`, `@modules/`, etc.)
- NO comments in code
- All functions need explicit return types
- Do NOT use `{ ...spread }` as shortcut

## Return
1. The new branch name
2. The PR URL
3. Summary of files changed

content.text: Now I have a complete picture of all files with `require-object-params` violations. Let me read all the affected files:
The violations in `mathematical-functions.util.ts` are on `private static` methods. Let me check if the ESLint rule applies to private methods or only exported/public ones.

content.text:
Now fix the `private formatValidationErrors` methods. These are `private` methods with a single param of type array. Let me check: the ESLint rule fires on them because they have a non-object positional param.

For the condensed-feature model, default-unit-price model, forecasted-trending-products model, product-annual-sales model, redshift-orders model, and redshift-trending-items model - they all have `private formatValidationErrors(errors: ValidationError[])` which needs `{ errors }` destructuring.

Let me check each of these and their call sites:

input.description: Find all formatValidationErrors call sites (non-object pattern)

content.text:
I see that in `default-unit-price.model.ts`, `formatValidationErrors` is already called with `{ errors }` (object pattern). Let me check which ones are violations and need to be fixed:

The violations are:
1. `condensed-feature.model.ts` line 322 - `formatValidationErrors(errors: ValidationError[])`
2. `forecasted-trending-products.model.ts` lines 418, 440
3. `product-annual-sales.model.ts` line 185
4. `redshift-orders.model.ts` line 69
5. `redshift-trending-items.model.ts` line 221
6. `default-unit-prices.service.ts` line 50 - `upsertUnitPrice`
7. `default-unit-price.model.ts` line 227 - `upsert`
8. `promotional-products.service.ts` line 95 - `getAllProducts`
9. `redshift-trending-items.service.ts` lines 166, 230, 266
10. `populate-style-copurchases.command.ts` line 100 - already fixed
11. `color-match-worker.service.ts` line 77 - already fixed

Let me now do the `formatValidationErrors` changes in all the model files. I'll use a Python script to do batch replacements:

input.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import re

files = [
    '/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service/src/modules/db/condensed-features/models/condensed-feature.model.ts',
    '/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service/src/modules/db/forecasted-trending-products/models/forecasted-trending-products.model.ts',
    '/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service/src/modules/db/product-annual-sales/models/product-annual-sales.model.ts',
    '/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service/src/modules/db/redshift/orders/models/redshift-orders.model.ts',
    '/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service/src/modules/db/redshift/trending-items/models/redshift-trending-items.model.ts',
]

for f in files:
    content = open(f).read()
    # Fix the method signature
    old_sig = 'private formatValidationErrors(errors: ValidationError[]): string {'
    new_sig = 'private formatValidationErrors({ errors }: { errors: ValidationError[] }): string {'
    if old_sig in content:
        content = content.replace(old_sig, new_sig)
        print(f'Fixed method sig in {f.split("/")[-1]}')
    
    # Fix all call sites in the same file - replace this.formatValidationErrors(errors) 
    # and this.formatValidationErrors(errors as ValidationError[])
    # and this.formatValidationErrors(responseErrors)
    content = content.replace('this.formatValidationErrors(errors as ValidationError[])', 'this.formatValidationErrors({ errors: errors as ValidationError[] })')
    content = content.replace('this.formatValidationErrors(errors)', 'this.formatValidationErrors({ errors })')
    content = content.replace('this.formatValidationErrors(responseErrors)', 'this.formatValidationErrors({ errors: responseErrors })')
    
    open(f, 'w').write(content)
    print(f'Done: {f.split("/")[-1]}')

EOF
